items.title: Terminal diagnosis hasn't stopped Joan Humble's final exhibition

children.content: After a lifetime of capturing Tasmanian landscapes, Joan Humble is holding her final exhibition. The 88-year-old has a terminal cancer diagnosis, but she is determined to keep painting as long as she can hold a brush.

gallery.altText: Woman sits on chair, using a magnifying glass to paint

thumbnail.altText: Woman sits on chair, using a magnifying glass to paint

items.title: Man in serious condition after being shot by Tasmania Police

children.content: A man is in a serious but stable condition after being shot by police during an altercation in Hobart's northern suburbs early on Sunday morning.

image.altText: Two police cars parked on a street with a drone in the air

items.title: 'Chock-a-block' cemetery means non-Catholics have no grave options

children.content: The Anglican church in a small northern Tasmanian town has exhausted all its cemetery space after selling off land to accommodate new social and affordable housing.

image.altText: A woman in a blue cardigan stands in a cemetery on a cloudy day.

items.title: Fuel crisis driving some farmers to consider abandoning harvests

children.content: Tasmanian farmers are considering not harvesting crops like broccoli and cauliflower because increasing freight costs are proving to be so debilitating.

image.altText: a tractor is parked next to a diesel fuel pump

items.title: From Hobart to the world stage: Theatre community mourns death of a giant

children.content: A giant of Australian theatre who helped launch the careers of some of the country's biggest names of the stage has died aged 93.

image.altText: A man in a suit looks at the camera.

items.title: More people are using this 'secret deal' to get an EV. Here's what to know

children.content: With the petrol bowser continuing to hurt wallets, more Australians than ever are considering purchasing an electric vehicle through a novated lease.

gallery.altText: Image shows a close-up view of a man's hand holding an electric vehicle charger and inserting it in the charging point.

thumbnail.altText: Image shows a close-up view of a man's hand holding an electric vehicle charger and inserting it in the charging point.

items.title: Japan assures Australia will get 'normal supply' of fuel

children.content: Fuel will continue to flow from Japan into Australia under assurances given during recent talks between the two countries.

image.altText: A woman in a blue suit laughs as she speaks into a microphone with a Japanese flag behind her.

items.title: Price crunch has some servo owners asking: 'Is it really worth doing this?'

children.content: Workers are reporting an increase in abuse from customers as service station owners say they're not making money from fuel price rises.

image.altText: External shots of a 7/11 petrol station and fuel pump with an out of order sign.

items.title: The cleanest air on Earth holds a powerful lesson about our climate

children.content: Perched on Tasmania's wild north-west coast, Cape Grim has been quietly tracking the world's cleanest air for 50 years. Its data tell an interesting story about how our world has changed over half a century, including rare success stories.

gallery.altText: A rocky shore in the foreground, with water and a large green cliff and mass of land in the background.
Graeme Pearman wears a red jacket and red flares, as he sets up a tower at Cape Grim in 1976

thumbnail.altText: A rocky shore in the foreground, with water and a large green cliff and mass of land in the background.
Graeme Pearman wears a red jacket and red flares, as he sets up a tower at Cape Grim in 1976

items.title: Fiji cruise ship runs aground after hitting reef near 'Cast Away island'

children.content: Thirty passengers have reportedly been rescued after a cruise ship ran aground on a reef near the island where the film Cast Away was shot.

image.altText: A three-level cruise ship.

items.title: The 'enshitification' of the system has left Australia's unis at a crossroad

children.content: A generation ago, studying in Australia opened doors. Today rising costs and falling satisfaction are making international students think twice, and locals feel sidelined.

image.altText: Graphic image hand throwing academic cap in air, against red circle, green background, black white photo of students

items.title: Dirty rentals are a 'common' experience for new tenants, but legal rights exist

children.content: Having to clean a new rental property before settling in is a common experience for renters, but landlords have obligations when it comes to rental cleanliness upon moving in.

gallery.altText: A wide-angle image of a clean and modern apartment with large windows, white kitchen cupboards and wooden floors
A young woman stands behind a kitchen islan which is covered in various items

thumbnail.altText: A wide-angle image of a clean and modern apartment with large windows, white kitchen cupboards and wooden floors
A young woman stands behind a kitchen islan which is covered in various items

avatar.alt: A headshot of a woman with long light brown hair and blue eyes

items.title: Bulldogs down plucky Bombers but lose two more players to injury

image.altText: An AFL player in red and blue is kneeled over in pain

children.content: The Western Bulldogs hold off an Essendon fight back to triumph by 34 points, but will sweat on the fitness of Artie Jones and Tim English, who both limped off at Docklands.

items.title: Raiders' season lurches towards disaster as Knights run riot

children.content: The reigning minor premiers are in freefall after Canberra slumped to a fourth-straight loss at the hands of a resurgent Knights outfit.

image.altText: A group of rugby league players in orange celebrate while players in green watch on

items.title: Lomax's false start adds to anticipation ahead of pro rugby debut

children.content: Fans will have to wait for Zac Lomax's Western Force debut, providing further intrigue about how the ex-NRL star will cope when he takes his first steps in professional rugby.

image.altText: Zac Lomax walking across Lang Park before the Force played the Reds.

items.title: A great gift or unexpected curse? Reflections on life as a new retiree

children.content: While time is retirement's great gift, it can become a curse for anyone who has devoted decades to a career and then suddenly wakes up with relevance deprivation syndrome and no firm plan on what comes next, writes Matt Eaton.

gallery.altText: Three men smile for a selfie. Two hold guitars while another holds up drumsticks while sat at a kit.

thumbnail.altText: Three men smile for a selfie. Two hold guitars while another holds up drumsticks while sat at a kit.

items.title: Quiz: How well do you know your history?

children.content: Where was the Venus de Milo found? And what common household item first went on sale in April 1827?

image.altText: photo of Venus de Milo statue

items.title: How close a tree is planted can affect property prices, new report shows

children.content: Tree canopy coverage is being boosted in every capital city, but one micro-level analysis has found a link between property prices and the proximity of trees to houses.

image.altText: Dark SUV driving down city street with lots of trees in it

items.title: The Kremlin's internet purge is like 'the Soviet era on steroids'

children.content: The Kremlin is increasing its efforts to dictate the narrative in Russia by shutting off the internet, restricting foreign messenger apps and punishing citizens for speaking out.

gallery.altText: close up of russian president vladimir putin during a meeting in the kremlin

thumbnail.altText: close up of russian president vladimir putin during a meeting in the kremlin

items.title: 'Abandoned': Sisters of officer slain by Dezi Freeman to sue Victoria Police

children.content: Leading Senior Constable Neal Thompson's sisters plan to launch legal action against Victoria Police over his death. They also say they felt "abandoned" by police after their brother's death.

image.altText: A 59-year-old man stands on a rural property with his dog looking happy.

items.title: Tim Tszyu dominates Denis Nurja to claim title in Wollongong

children.content: Tim Tszyu has won the WBO international middleweight title with a lopsided unanimous points win over Albanian Denis Nurja in Wollongong.

image.altText: A shirtless boxer with a shaved head has his hand held up in victory, title belt next to him

items.title: Kings break NBL trophy after dramatic overtime win as 36ers blow late lead

children.content: After trailing by six points late in regulation time, a dominant overtime display has earned the Sydney Kings a 113-101 victory in the fifth and deciding match of the NBL Finals.

image.altText: Sydney Kings player Torrey Craig holds two figurines of basketball players after they fell off the NBL championship trophy.

items.title: Police look for missing cocaine after ocean 'drop-off' during 'bizarre' journey

children.content: Setting off from Panama just before Christmas, police say the MV Raider started its journey towards Sydney with almost 6 tonnes of cocaine aboard worth nearly $2 billion.

image.altText: A helicoper flies over a ship carrying drugs

items.title: Queen at 100 is honoured in fashion exhibition as Andrew scandal bites

children.content: Within palace walls the Queen was a godlike figure. As her glorious fashion goes on display to commemorate what would have been her 100th birthday, how would she have responded to the Andrew crisis if alive today?

gallery.altText: Queen Elizabeth II in a long formal blue gown with bolero jacket and headpiece sitting on a red velvet seat with gold trim

thumbnail.altText: Queen Elizabeth II in a long formal blue gown with bolero jacket and headpiece sitting on a red velvet seat with gold trim

items.title: Trash or treasure? Albanese's big gamble

children.content: Labor's landslide re-election brought renewed hope of reform, but in the past 12 months many anti-gambling advocates only became further disillusioned.

image.altText: Side on close up of Anthony Albanese, not speaking

items.title: Stagflation is already happening, a leading economist warns

children.content: With stagflation risks rising, what lessons could the treasurer learn from the past?

image.altText: An empty sign on petrol pumps

items.title: Australians find joy amid global uncertainty in Easter celebrations

children.content: Christians of all denominations gather at churches and cathedrals to mark Easter and reflect on their faith amid recent global turmoil.

articles.title: Aerial photographer's hunt for 'Easter eggs' yields stunning images

children.content: Scott McCook spends his spare time photographing the salt lakes of WA's Wheatbelt, which look uncannily like giant eggs when viewed from the air.

image.altText: A orange salt lake surrounded by red dirt

gallery.altText: A service at St Pauls Cathedral

thumbnail.altText: A service at St Pauls Cathedral

items.title: Magnitude-5.5 earthquake 'morning wake-up call' to remote community

children.content: A remote community in the Anangu Pitjantjatjara Yankunytjatjara (APY) Lands have woken up to rattling shelves and swinging lights caused by a magnitude-5.5 earthquake, Geoscience Australia says.

image.altText: A red dirt football oval next to a grassed football oval and a small community with a mountain range in the background

items.title: 'Doesn't make any sense': Young Aussies can't justify fuel costs to get to work

children.content: Young people are taking extraordinary steps to deal with the fuel crisis, including cutting back on work because the cost of fuel to get there is now higher than the money they will earn for the shift.

image.altText: A petrol station

items.title: Artemis crew enter day four with plans to take manual control of spacecraft

children.content: The Artemis astronauts have been taking a moment to look back at Earth as they continue deep into space toward the Moon.

gallery.altText: A women peers out of a spacecraft cabin window, looking down at Earth. Her long hair floats upwards in the zero gravity.

thumbnail.altText: A women peers out of a spacecraft cabin window, looking down at Earth. Her long hair floats upwards in the zero gravity.

items.title: Travellers celebrate as major highway reopens after Cyclone Narelle

children.content: Holiday makers took a gamble heading to WA's north hoping a major highway would reopen in time for Easter.

image.altText: Four men play cricket on a road with a boat and caravan parked nearby.

items.title: 'Struggling massively': Farmer finds it hard to put food on the table

children.content: Soaring fuel costs and interest rates are forcing some South Australians to change their travel plans as a small producer says he has struggled to put food on the table.

image.altText: A man stands behind a glass refrigerated counter under a marquee. A sign Lakeview Acres Farm hung behind him

items.title: Whole villages in Sumatra erased overnight — and still living in the wreckage

children.content: Months after floods killed more than a thousand people across Sumatra, residents are still digging their way out of the wreckage. While survivors are eager to rebuild, many just can’t shake the fear that the next catastrophe is just around the corner.

gallery.altText: A lone house stands in the middle of debris and logs in Sawang district.
Drone footage showing seas of timber throughout Geudumbak — an area east of Sawang district in North Aceh.
A sea of timber running throughout North Aceh after the floods.

thumbnail.altText: A lone house stands in the middle of debris and logs in Sawang district.
Drone footage showing seas of timber throughout Geudumbak — an area east of Sawang district in North Aceh.
A sea of timber running throughout North Aceh after the floods.

items.title: Motorists urged to dob in service stations over fuel price hikes

children.content: The NSW government fast-tracks a compliance blitz to strengthen fuel price transparency and protect motorists from misleading pricing.

image.altText: a NSW Fair Trading officer holding a mobile phone carrying out an inspection

items.title: Huge sea turtle rescued after being discovered stranded upside down

children.content: A large sea turtle, estimated to be at least 30 years old and weighing more than 50 kilograms, is recovering in a wildlife sanctuary after being spotted stuck in dunes on a northern NSW beach.

gallery.altText: A large turtle on the sand at a beach.

thumbnail.altText: A large turtle on the sand at a beach.
